message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): 3xx redirect current-behavior snapshot + security note (tick 349)

Added `distributed_http_client_3xx_redirect_current_behavior_snapshot`
— a snapshot test documenting + pinning the current 3xx-redirect
behavior of `DistributedHttpClient`.

Discovery during development: the client (built on the default
asupersync HTTP client config) **follows 3xx redirects transparently**.
A 302 with `Location: http://does-not-resolve.invalid./` causes the
client to attempt a connect to the unresolvable location, which
returns `Err(ConnectError-shaped error)` — not `Ok(302)` with the
redirect visible to the caller.

**SECURITY NOTE** recorded in the test doc comment: transparent
redirect following is a real concern for a distributed RPC client
where URLs are supposed to point at trusted peer nodes. A compromised
peer could respond 302 with `Location: http://attacker.com/` and
exfiltrate the next request's body. The fix is to configure
`RedirectPolicy::none()` at the `DistributedHttpClient` construction
site, but that's policy-level — a follow-up bead, not ft-xbnl0.2.4
scope (this bead migrates OFF tokio, it doesn't change default
policy).

The test pins the status quo so:
1. Any caller relying on the current "Err on bad redirect" behavior
   (implicit treat-as-unreachable) stays working.
2. A future commit that changes RedirectPolicy to none() will cause
   this test to fail — that's the intended trigger to update the test
   to assert `resp.status == 302` for the security-improved path and
   file a separate bead to close out the security concern.

Test result: 1 passed; 0 failed
Compile time: 1m 14s cold.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
